title: bubonium aquaticum subsp. nanus
intro: Bubonium aquaticum subsp. nanus, also known as dwarf water buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Bubonium aquaticum subsp. nanus include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and providing relief from coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and ointments for topical application.
